task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is open, and the title bar, xsheet, or browser shows 'dwanko_run.tnz' loaded.

task_completion.description: Apply a transparency or fade-out effect to the character.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX Schematic or Xsheet shows a Transparency/Opacity effect node connected to the character column, or the column's opacity settings are being manipulated.

task_completion.description: Set the character to be 100% visible at Frame 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A keyframe is visible at Frame 1 in the timeline/xsheet for the transparency/opacity effect, with the value set to fully opaque (e.g., 100, 255, or 0% transparency).

task_completion.description: Set the character to be 100% invisible at Frame 20.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A keyframe is visible at Frame 20 in the timeline/xsheet for the transparency/opacity effect, with the value set to fully transparent (e.g., 0, or 100% transparency).

task_completion.description: Configure render settings for PNG sequence with alpha channel support.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window is open, showing the file format set to 'png' (or similar image sequence format) and the alpha channel option enabled (e.g., 'RGBM', 'RGBA', or 'Alpha' checkbox ticked).

task_completion.description: Render frames 1 through 20 to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings show the save path as '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/fade_out/' and the frame range set from 1 to 20. A render progress bar or a file browser showing the resulting 20 PNG files in the target directory should be visible.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz interface (menus, timeline, schematic, output settings) rather than just terminal commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The PNG files were generated via OpenToonz's render engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Render' button in OpenToonz and a render progress window appears, rather than using a script like ImageMagick or ffmpeg to generate fake blank images.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create empty files to bypass rendering.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal commands showing 'touch' or 'cp' being used to artificially populate the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/fade_out/' directory with dummy PNG files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The output directory must be '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/fade_out'. The rendered sequence must contain at least 20 PNG files representing frames 1 to 20. Frame 1 must be fully opaque (alpha > 200) and Frame 20 must be fully transparent (alpha < 10).
